title: [cm/unique-constraint] entityBundleFieldInfo

given: two records that each satisfy a server-side uniqueness invariant in isolation
when: both are created independently (offline / concurrent replicas)
then: the port's convergence rule for the uniqueness violation is observed and asserted

why_non_obvious: intent-CM graded this unique-constraint site 'hard': [unique-constraint] The `UniqueBirthLog` constraint assumes globally serialized uniqueness for the asset field, which cannot be guaranteed under eventual consistency without coordination or a chosen conflict-resolution strategy.  [also surfaced by source-read: A constraint named UniqueBirthLog suggests invariant enforcement on every write, but the validator returns without checking any non-new log.]  [also surfaced by source-read: Read-side queries elsewhere apply access checks, and a port might naturally enforce uniqueness only against visible records; this validator explicitly disables ]  [also surfaced by source-read: The synchronization is triggered on both insert and update, while the log's asset reference is not visibly restricted here to animal children; the date update i]  [also surfaced by source-read: The feature appears to link an animal to its birth log, and uniqueness is intended, but existing inconsistent data can yield multiple IDs and the query has no o]

source_read.rule: The animal birthdate display links to an arbitrary first accessible birth log referencing that asset, rather than selecting by timestamp, creation time, or an explicitly defined canonical ordering.

title: [source] A specified mother is added to a referenced child's parent field only when that

given: A birth log specifies mother M and references child C.
C already has at least one parent reference, which is not M.
when: The birth log is inserted or updated.
then: M is not appended to C's parent field.
C's existing parent references remain unchanged.

why_non_obvious: A field called mother can suggest adding or reconciling maternal lineage, but the implementation treats any existing parent reference as a complete veto.

source_read.rule: A specified mother is added to a referenced child's parent field only when that child currently has no parents at all; it is not added alongside an existing parent and does not replace one.

title: [source] Animal nicknames are an ordered multi-value collection, not a single nickname st

given: An animal has more than one nickname.
when: The animal is stored and later read.
then: All nickname values are retained as values of the same field rather than one replacing the others.

why_non_obvious: The singular-looking field name `nickname` and ordinary string type can easily lead to a scalar implementation; the source explicitly permits multiple values.

source_read.rule: Animal nicknames are an ordered multi-value collection, not a single nickname string.

title: [source] An animal must have exactly one required species/breed taxonomy reference in the

given: An animal is being created or updated.
A species/breed value is supplied that does not yet exist as a taxonomy term.
when: The animal is saved with that species/breed value.
then: The value is represented as a required reference to an `animal_type` taxonomy term.
A new referenced taxonomy term may be created rather than rejecting the unknown value.

why_non_obvious: The field label suggests free-form species/breed text, and a port could model it as an optional string or a reference to a fixed pre-seeded species list. Instead it is required, taxonomy-backed, and allows auto-creation.

source_read.rule: An animal must have exactly one required species/breed taxonomy reference in the `animal_type` vocabulary; creating a referenced term on demand is permitted.

title: [source] Animal sex is restricted to the stored codes `F` and `M`, whose displayed meanin

given: An animal sex value is assigned.
when: The value is validated or materialized.
then: Only `F` (Female) or `M` (Male) is a defined value.
The persisted/domain value is the code, not necessarily the display label.

why_non_obvious: A port may persist display labels, use expanded values such as `female`/`male`, or infer a broader/unconstrained gender model from the field name. The source defines a closed two-code value set.

source_read.rule: Animal sex is restricted to the stored codes `F` and `M`, whose displayed meanings are Female and Male.

title: [source] The canonical animal sterility field is `is_sterile`; historical `is_castrated`

given: Existing animal field data exists under `is_castrated`, potentially across revisions, translations, deltas, or deleted rows.
then: Each old stored value is preserved under `is_sterile` with its corresponding entity/revision/language/delta context.
`is_castrated` is no longer the retained canonical field after migration.

why_non_obvious: A simple rename implementation could retain an alias, migrate only current entity state, or collapse revisions/translations. The migration copies the complete field-row identity including entity, revision, language, delta, bundle, and deletion status, then uninstalls the old field.

source_read.rule: The canonical animal sterility field is `is_sterile`; historical `is_castrated` values are copied revision-for-revision and language-for-language before the old field is removed.

title: [hook-implementation] FieldHooks

given: the entity state the hook keys on
when: the lifecycle event that triggers the hook occurs
then: the hook's derived side effect is observable at the value boundary

why_non_obvious: A hook fires a side effect on an entity lifecycle event. A re-implementer who models only the primary write can miss the derived state the hook maintains.

title: [validation-constraint] UniqueBirthLogConstraint

given: two records that would each independently satisfy creation
when: both are created (concurrently / offline) so both violate the constraint
then: the system's convergence behavior for the constraint is observed and asserted

why_non_obvious: A server-side validation constraint enforces an invariant at write time. A port over an append-only/eventual store cannot enforce it globally and must pick an explicit resolution — the semantic no method name conveys.
